title: Ormosia steyermarkii
taxonomy: Ormosia steyermarkii is classified within the plant family Leguminosae, also known as Fabaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of plants, commonly referred to as the legume family. Leguminosae are characterized by the production of fruits that are pods, and they often have nodules on their roots that contain nitrogen-fixing bacteria. Ormosia steyermarkii is a species of flowering plant in the genus Ormosia, which is native to Central and South America. The classification of Ormosia steyermarkii is also further detailed in the subfamily Faboideae.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
